F01.title: Partial dead-letter write leaves an orphan active file that poisons all future dead-lettering (within-run create_new collision)

F02.title: Drain confirms+deletes a segment without verifying CommitResult covers every input record (committed + dead_lettered may not partition the batch)

F03.title: Single permanently-rejected batch larger than dead_letter_max_bytes wedges the drain in a permanent block↔retry livelock

F04.title: Sink::health() is called with no timeout backstop; a hanging health() stalls the entire drain

F05.title: Retried multi-batch segment re-dead-letters earlier sub-batches, amplifying duplicate dead-letter files and thrashing the cap

F06.title: Dead-letter total_bytes silently undercounts on metadata() failure, bypassing the dead_letter_max_bytes cap

F07.title: Dead-letter cap accounting omits fixed 60-byte per-file segment overhead, so dead_letter_max_bytes can be exceeded

F08.title: Swallowed rescan() error can wedge the drain blocked despite an operator-freed dead-letter directory

F09.title: dead_letter_full counter and blocked_since reset on every unblock→reblock cycle, inflating the metric and resetting blocked-duration

F10.title: .confirmed sidecar created without explicit 0o600 mode, tripping the daemon's own recovery mode audit under any non-0o077 umask

F11.title: DeadLetterWriter has zero unit tests; scan_dir counter-recovery across restart is untested

F12.title: Segment counter resets to 1 after crash recovery; the new run's seal()-rename silently overwrites a still-undrained recovered .wab.sealed segment (data loss, crown-invariant violation)

F14.title: recover_segment renames .wab -> .wab.sealed without fsync_parent_dir, unlike WabSegment::seal — recovered seal not crash-durable

F15.title: replay_unconfirmed silently skips sealed-but-unconfirmed segments on a per-entry DirEntry error (filter_map(|e| e.ok()))

F16.title: recover_open_segments processes the dead_letter/ directory as if it were a shard directory

F17.title: Recovery: no test for the oversized-payload_len boundary (record at exactly MAX_PAYLOAD_HARD_CAP must survive; one over must truncate)

F18.title: Recovery: the partial-seal sentinel branch during crash recovery is untested

F19.title: macOS data fsync uses F_BARRIERFSYNC (barrier) while directory durability uses plain fsync via sync_all — weaker than F_FULLFSYNC; an explicit, undertested tradeoff on the crown durability path

F20.title: Metrics HTTP server has no read/write timeout — slowloris permanently wedges the unauthenticated endpoint and blinds monitoring

F21.title: Phase-2 coalesce starves co-located shards on a shared worker (worker_count < shard_count)

F22.title: accept_latency histogram uses 1ms-floor buckets for a sub-millisecond measurement

F23.title: flush_shard 'WAB is shutting down' comment misdescribes the permanent-shard-offline case

F24.title: QueueSender exposes len() with no is_empty()

F25.title: UnknownMessageType/UnknownDurability decode errors are nacked as the (documented-as-transient, keep-connection-open) InternalError reason, yet the connection is closed

F26.title: Payload and CRC reads are not raced against handler_shutdown, so a mid-frame stall holds a semaphore permit until read_timeout during graceful shutdown

F27.title: Payload/CRC read timeout is a whole-transfer deadline but is documented and metered as a per-byte idle timeout

F28.title: Shutdown-time socket removal is path-based (std::fs::remove_file), inconsistent with the hardened unlinkat-based startup cleanup

F29.title: TLS handshake is not raced against handler_shutdown, so in-flight handshakes block graceful drain up to handshake_timeout

F30.title: umask save/restore in bind_hardened has no RAII guard; an unwind between set and restore would leak the tightened 0o177 umask process-wide

F31.title: MySQL sink dead-letters live data on a recoverable connection-time auth/access failure (asymmetric with Postgres)

F32.title: ClickHouse response body read is not bounded by the sink's configured timeout (error path can hang to the drain backstop)

F33.title: ClickHouse split_credentials leaves username-only userinfo in base_url, contradicting the struct invariant

F34.title: ClickHouse does not percent-decode URL credentials before HTTP basic auth (silent divergence from SQL sinks)

F35.title: ClickHouse content-derived dedup token silently breaks idempotency if sink_max_batch_size changes across a restart

F36.title: ClickHouse RowBinary encoding silently assumes a plain String column with no config validation and contract buried in a private fn comment

F37.title: ClickHouse sink commit() request/response classification has no in-process test (only an #[ignore] live-docker integration test)

F38.title: HTTP concurrency: no test asserts a transient mid-batch error actually cancels still-in-flight POSTs

F39.title: HTTP concurrency: no test pins record-to-outcome pairing (right payload in committed vs dead_lettered) under concurrent POSTs

F40.title: weir-ctl `dl list` / `dl drop` silently see zero dead-letter segments (wrong suffix filter)

F41.title: CommitResult partition invariant (committed ∪ dead_lettered = batch) is unenforced; the drain confirms+deletes the segment unconditionally on Ok

F42.title: SinkRecord::into_payload is documented as the dead-letter recovery path but is bypassed for whole-batch permanent (and transient) errors

F43.title: Blocking client sets no socket read/write/connect timeouts; a wedged daemon blocks producers indefinitely

F44.title: Client read_response allocates vec![0u8; payload_len] without the MAX_PAYLOAD_HARD_CAP guard the server applies before allocating

F45.title: weir-sink-sdk re-export doc describes Payload as bytes::Bytes, contradicting the R1 newtype rationale

F46.title: SDK doc overstates that health() is called after every commit attempt

F47.title: Client discards the daemon-version byte the server sends on a VersionMismatch Nack

F48.title: Public error enums and CommitResult are not #[non_exhaustive]; adding any variant/field after 1.0 is a breaking change

F49.title: Envelope::new silently truncates payload length via payload.len() as u32, falsifying the documented 'cannot desync' invariant

F50.title: Header::new takes a payload_len argument that Envelope::new always overwrites — an API that invites desync

F51.title: Payload newtype: PartialEq impls and the Borrow<[u8]> hashmap-key contract are untested

F52.title: Reserved flags byte is preserved verbatim on decode but never validated to be zero, foreclosing in-version flag evolution

F53.title: shard_count in 65..=256 with defaulted worker_count rejects a documented, in-range config with a misleading error

F54.title: Config-time warn! calls are silently dropped (tracing subscriber initialized after Config::load)

F55.title: Feature-gated [server] keys are silently dropped: in KNOWN_SERVER_KEYS but absent from RawConfig on builds without the feature

F56.title: HELP advertises feature-gated CLI flags that fail as generic 'unknown arguments' when the feature is off, with no feature hint

F57.title: Boolean env/CLI values accept only exactly 'true'/'false'; '1', '0', 'TRUE' abort startup

F58.title: log_level is never validated; invalid or empty values silently degrade or disable logging

F59.title: Config derives Debug, exposing sink_url credentials, inconsistent with the project's deliberate bearer_token redaction

F60.title: u64 durations range-checked via `as usize` truncate on 32-bit targets, letting out-of-range values pass
